title: Support rise/fall delays and min/typ/max delay
body:
This PR adds support for rise/fall delays by using the existing fork-token approach, just modifying it by adding a condition modifying the delay.
```verilog
#(captured_RHS==0 ? fallDelay : riseDelay)
```

> 10.3.3 Continuous assignment delays
> 
> A delay given to a continuous assignment shall specify the time duration between a right-hand operand value change and the assignment made to the left-hand side. If the left-hand references a scalar net, then the
delay shall be treated in the same way as for gate delays; that is, different delays can be given for the output rising, falling, and changing to high impedance (see 28.16).
> 
> If the left-hand references a vector net, then up to three delays can be applied. The following rules determine
which delay controls the assignment:
> 
> — If the right-hand side makes a transition from nonzero to zero, then the falling delay shall be used.
> 
> — If the right-hand side makes a transition to z, then the turn-off delay shall be used.
> 
> — For all other cases, the rising delay shall be used.

This means that even for a wide vector of bits, only the transition to `'0` should use the fall delay, others should use the rise delay.
Please note that this leaves turn-off delays unsupported, as there is no real 4-state support - when there is 4-state support, it can easily be extended.
